paper_title: Cave Monitoring and Sample Analysis Methods

methods.description: Description of the regular monitoring program conducted in Mechara caves, including the types of samples collected and parameters measured during field expeditions.

steps.description: Regularly monitor many of the Mechara caves since our first sampling expedition to the region in April 2004.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct regular field expeditions to cave sites for monitoring and sampling.

steps.description: Collected close to 100 surface water, groundwater and rainwater samples, and soda straw stalactites for isotopic and geochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect various water samples (surface, ground, rain) and soda straw stalactites.

steps.description: Measured in situ chemical parameters (conductivity, TDS, and pH), cave temperature and humidity.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure chemical parameters (conductivity, TDS, pH) and climate (temperature, humidity) in situ within the cave.

steps.description: Collected more than 30 relict and actively growing stalagmites.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect stalagmite samples (relict and actively growing).

steps.description: Analyzed the rainfall and temperature data analyzed from three National Meteorological stations at Mechara, Gelemso and Bedessa towns.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain and analyze meteorological data (rainfall, temperature) from nearby stations.
inputs: Meteorological station data (rainfall, temperature) from Mechara, Gelemso, Bedessa

methods.description: Detailed procedures for measuring temperature and humidity within the caves using handheld devices during visits and continuous loggers for long-term monitoring.

steps.description: Cave climate data has been obtained using Kestrel handheld weather stations during all visits to the caves.
steps.searchable_summary: Record cave climate data using handheld weather stations during field visits.

steps.description: Handheld stations were used to record relative humidity at different locations within the cave: results are accurate to ±3% relative humidity, with an upper measurement limit of 95%.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure relative humidity at multiple locations within the cave using handheld stations.
outputs: Relative humidity readings at different locations

steps.description: Tiny tag temperature loggers have been left in five caves (Rukiessa, Bero, Achere, Aynage and Goda Mea).
steps.searchable_summary: Deploy continuous temperature loggers in specific caves.
inputs: Specific cave locations (Rukiessa, Bero, Achere, Aynage, Goda Mea)

steps.description: Continuous cave temperature measurements have been recorded at 30 minutes interval through time. Loggers have a quoted accuracy of 0.25 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect continuous temperature data recorded by loggers at 30-minute intervals.

methods.description: Procedure for collecting surface water and cave water samples using HDPE bottles.

steps.description: Surface water samples (rivers and springs) and cave water samples (stagnant pools, flowing cave streams and drip waters) were collected in 60 or 30 ml HDPE bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface and cave water samples in 60 or 30 ml HDPE bottles.
inputs: Cave water sources (stagnant pools, flowing streams, drip waters)

methods.description: Laboratory analysis of collected water samples for pH, electrical conductivity, and TDS using specific multimeters.

steps.description: On return from the field, samples were analyzed for pH... using a WTW 340i Multimeter or Myron Ultrameter II-6P with an accuracy of ±0.1 pH.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure pH of collected water samples using a multimeter.

steps.description: On return from the field, samples were analyzed for... electrical conductivity and TDS using a WTW 340i Multimeter or Myron Ultrameter II-6P with an accuracy of... ±0.1% of reading for electrical conductivity and TDS.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure electrical conductivity and TDS of collected water samples using a multimeter.
outputs: Electrical conductivity values of water samples

methods.description: Preparation step for water samples specifically for subsequent isotopic analysis.

steps.description: Samples for isotope analysis were then immediately transferred to fill an 8 ml HDPE bottle.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer water samples into 8 ml HDPE bottles for isotope analysis.
outputs: Water samples in 8 ml HDPE bottles

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing water samples for oxygen isotopes using CO2 equilibration and mass spectrometry.

steps.description: For isotopic analysis, the waters were equilibrated with CO2 using an Isoprep 18 device for oxygen isotope analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Equilibrate water samples with CO2 using an Isoprep 18 device.

steps.description: ...with mass spectrometry performed on a VG SIRA.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform mass spectrometry on equilibrated CO2 samples using a VG SIRA.

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing water samples for hydrogen isotopes using on-line Cr reduction and mass spectrometry.

steps.description: For hydrogen isotope analysis, an on-line Cr reduction method was used...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform on-line Cr reduction on water samples for hydrogen isotope analysis.

steps.description: ...with either a EuroPyrOH-3110 system coupled to a Micromass Isoprime mass spectrometer or a GV
steps.searchable_summary: Perform mass spectrometry on hydrogen gas samples using a Micromass Isoprime or GV mass spectrometer.
inputs: Micromass Isoprime mass spectrometer (coupled to EuroPyrOH-3110)
